grant_title: Rhode Island State Council on the Arts (RISCA) General Grant Opportunities
deadline: April 1 by 11:59 p.m. (for multiple grants including Project Grants for Organizations, Project Grants in Education, Arts and Health Grants, Arts and Health Incubator Grants, Community Engaged Project Grants, Make Art Grants, Folk Arts Apprenticeships, and Folk Arts Fellowships); September 1 by 11:59 p.m. (for General Operating Support for Artists and General Operating Support for Organizations)
eligible_applicant_type: Organizations with a fiscal sponsor (nonprofit with 501(c)(3) status registered in Rhode Island)
project_area: Arts and culture, arts education, arts and health, folk arts fellowships and apprenticeships, community engaged projects, and general operating support for artists and organizations
demographic_requirements: No specific demographic requirements beyond being based in Rhode Island or having a Rhode Island-based fiscal sponsor
eligibility_summary: Open to artists, schools, and 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations based in Rhode Island. Organizations without 501(c)(3) status may apply using a Rhode Island-based fiscal sponsor with 501(c)(3) status. Some grants allow multiple applications per cycle (e.g., Arts and Health Incubator Grants).
